mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within two weeks.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over individual series in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates capacity utilization improvements without pricing pressure, generating positive short-term equity returns as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized, or Rank IC turns negative during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which consistently predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation accelerates above 2.5% annually, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >50% of months following CPIAUCSL breaches above 2.5%, or the signal loses significance after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong retail sales growth and weak housing starts indicates consumption-driven demand that lacks durable goods support, leading to negative equity returns as earnings sustainability is questioned.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, weak payroll growth indicates fragile labor demand and subdued consumer spending, which suppresses short-term equity returns as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
